passages.title: Bamboo, a Wonder Plant
passages.source_html:
Bamboo, a Wonder Plant 


Bamboo is used for a wide range of purposes, but now it seems it may be under threat. 


A


Every year, during the rainy season, the mountain gorillas of central Africa migrate to the lower slopes of the Virunga Mountains to graze on bamboo. For the 650 or so that remain in the wild, it’s a vital food source. Without it, says Ian Redmond, chairman of the Ape Alliance, their chances of survival would be reduced significantly. Gorillas aren’t the only local keen on bamboo. For the people who live close to the Virungas, it’s a valuable and versatile raw material. But in the past 100 years or so, resources have come under increasing pressure as populations have exploded and large areas of bamboo forest have been cleared to make way for commercial plantations. Sadly, this isn’t an isolated story. All over the world, the ranges of many bamboo species appear to be shrinking, endangering the people and animals that depend upon them. 


B


Despite bamboo’s importance, we know surprisingly little about it. A recent report published by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) and the International Network for Bamboo and Rattan (INBAR) has revealed just how profound our ignorance of global bamboo resources is, particularly in relation to conservation. There are almost 1,600 recognised species of bamboo, but the report concentrated on the 1,200 or so woody varieties distinguished by the strong stems, or ‘culms’, that most people associate with this versatile plant. Of these, only 38 ‘priority species’ identified for their commercial value have been the subject of any real scientific research to date. This problem isn’t confined to bamboo. Compared to the work carried out on animals, the science of assessing the conservation status of plants is still in its infancy. ‘People have only started looking at this during the past 10-15 years, and only now are they understanding how to go about it systematically,’ says Dr Valerie Kapos, one of the report’s authors. 


C


Bamboo tends to grow in ‘stands’ (or groups) made up of individual plants that grow from roots known as rhizomes. It is the world’s fastest-growing woody plant and some species grow over a meter in one day. But the plant’s ecological role extends beyond providing food for wildlife. Its rhizome systems, which lie in the top layers of the soil, are crucial in preventing soil erosion. And there is growing evidence that bamboo plays an important part in determining forest structure and dynamics. ‘Bamboo’s pattern of mass flowering and mass death leaves behind large areas of dry biomass that attract wildfire,’ says Kapos, ‘When these bum, they create patches of open ground far bigger than would be left by a fallen tree. Patchiness helps to preserve diversity because certain plant species do better during the early stages of regeneration when there are gaps in the canopy.’ 


D


However, bamboo’s most immediate significance lies in its economic value. Many countries, particularly in Asia, are involved in the trade of bamboo products. Modern processing techniques mean it can be used in a variety of ways, for example as flooring and laminates. Traditionally it is used in construction, but one of the fastest growing bamboo products is paper – 25 per cent of paper produced in India is made from bamboo fibre. Of course, bamboo’s main function has always been in domestic applications, and as a locally traded product, it is worth about US$4.5 billion annually. Bamboo is often the only readily available raw material for people in many developing countries, says Chris Stapleton, a research associate at the UK’s Royal Botanic Gardens. ‘Bamboo can be harvested from forest areas or grown quickly elsewhere, and then converted simply without expensive machinery or facilities,’ he says, ‘In this way, it contributes substantially to poverty alleviation.’ 


E


Keen horticulturists will spot an apparent contradiction in the worrying picture painted by the UNEP-INBAR report. Those in the West who’ve followed the recent vogue for cultivating exotic species in their gardens will point out that, if it isn’t kept in check, bamboo can cause real problems. ‘In a lot of places, the people who live with bamboo don’t perceive it as being under threat in any way,’ says Kapos, ‘In fact, a lot of bamboo species are very invasive if they’ve been introduced.’ So why are so many species endangered? ‘There are two separate issues here’, says Ray Townsend, arboretum manager at the Royal Botanic Gardens, ‘Some plants are threatened because they can’t survive in the habitat – they aren’t strong enough or there aren’t enough of them, perhaps. But bamboo can take care of itself – it’s strong enough to survive if left alone. What is under threat is its habitat. When forest goes, it’s converted into something else; then there isn’t anywhere for forest plants such as bamboo to grow.’


F 


Around the world, bamboo species are routinely protected as part of the forest ecosystem in national parks and reserves, but there is next to nothing that protects bamboo in the wild for its own sake. The UNEP-INBAR report will help conservationists to establish effective measures aimed at protecting valuable wild bamboo species. Townsend, too, sees the UNEP-INBAR report as an important step forward in promoting the cause of bamboo conservation. ‘Until now, bamboo has been perceived as a second-class plant. When you talk about places like the Amazon, everyone always thinks about hardwoods. Of course, these are significant but there’s a tendency to overlook the plants they are associated with, which are often bamboo species.’

groups.title: Questions 1-13
groups.source_html:
Questions 1-7


The passage has six sections, A-F. Which section contains the following information? Write the correct letter, A-F, in boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet. 


NB You may use any letter more than once.

 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
1

1 an assessment of current levels of knowledge about bamboo
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Despite bamboo’s importance, we know surprisingly little about it. A recent report published by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) and the International Network for Bamboo and Rattan (INBAR) has revealed just how profound our ignorance of global bamboo resources is, particularly in relation to conservation.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落包含了评估当前人们对竹子了解多少。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的"assessment of current levels of knowledge about bamboo"定位到B段首句"Despite bamboo’s importance, we know surprisingly little about it"，这句话直接表明人们对竹子的了解很少，紧接着第二句提到一份报告揭示了我们对全球竹资源的认知是多么的匮乏。

解题思路：原文B段明确提到"we know surprisingly little about it"（我们对它的了解少得惊人），并且提到一份报告揭示了"how profound our ignorance of global bamboo resources is"（我们对全球竹资源的认知是多么的匮乏），这正对应题目中"an assessment of current levels of knowledge about bamboo"（评估当前人们对竹子了解多少）的要求。因此B段包含了题目所问的信息。
2

2 a comparison between bamboo and more fragile plants
✅ 正确答案：E

原文答案句：There are two separate issues here’, says Ray Townsend, arboretum manager at the Royal Botanic Gardens, ‘Some plants are threatened because they can’t survive in the habitat – they aren’t strong enough or there aren’t enough of them, perhaps. But bamboo can take care of itself – it’s strong enough to survive if left alone.

对应段落：E段

审题：题目要求找出竹子与更脆弱植物对比的段落。

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"comparison between bamboo and more fragile plants"定位到E段，该段明确提到了竹子与其他植物的对比，指出竹子比其他植物更强壮。

解题思路：E段中Ray Townsend明确对比了竹子与其他植物的生存能力，指出竹子比其他植物更强壮，能够自我保护，而其他植物则因为生存能力弱而面临威胁，这正好符合题目要求，因此，选择E段。
3

3 details of the commercial significance of bamboo
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：However, bamboo’s most immediate significance lies in its economic value. Many countries, particularly in Asia, are involved in the trade of bamboo products. Modern processing techniques mean it can be used in a variety of ways, for example as flooring and laminates. Traditionally it is used in construction, but one of the fastest growing bamboo products is paper – 25 per cent of paper produced in India is made from bamboo fibre.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是哪个段落包含“关于竹子的商业重要性的三个细节”。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的“commercial significance”（商业重要性）定位到文章中关于竹子经济价值的描述。

解题思路：D段明确提到了竹子的经济价值，包括竹制品贸易、现代加工技术的应用（如地板和层压板）、传统用途（建筑）以及快速增长的产品（纸张），这些都是竹子商业重要性的具体细节。因此，答案为D。
4

4 a human development that is threatening the availability of bamboo
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：But in the past 100 years or so, resources have come under increasing pressure as populations have exploded and large areas of bamboo forest have been cleared to make way for commercial plantations. 

对应段落：A段  

审题：题目问的是哪个段落包含“某项人类活动正危及竹资源的可持续供应。”  

定位关键信息：通过题干中的关键词"threatening the availability of bamboo"和"human development"可以定位到A段中描述人类活动对竹子造成威胁的句子。  

解题思路：A段明确提到"large areas of bamboo forest have been cleared to make way for commercial plantations"，即大面积的竹林被清除，以腾出空间用于商业种植园，这正是人类发展活动对竹资源的可持续性造成的威胁。因此答案为A。
5

5 a description of the limited extent of existing research on bamboo
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：Despite bamboo’s importance, we know surprisingly little about it. A recent report published by the UN Environment Programme (UNEP) and the International Network for Bamboo and Rattan (INBAR) has revealed just how profound our ignorance of global bamboo resources is, particularly in relation to conservation.

对应段落：B段

审题：题目要求找出描述现有竹类研究有限的段落。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词"limited extent of existing research"（现有研究的有限范围）定位到B段首句"Despite bamboo’s importance, we know surprisingly little about it"（尽管竹子很重要，但我们对其了解惊人地少）。

解题思路：B段明确提到"we know surprisingly little about it"（我们对它了解很少）和"our ignorance of global bamboo resources is...profound"（我们对全球竹资源的认知是多么的匮乏），直接对应题目中"limited extent of existing research"（现有研究的有限）。此外，该段还提到"only 38 ‘priority species’ identified for their commercial value have been the subject of any real scientific research to date"（目前只有38种被列为商业价值的优先物种受到真正的科学研究），进一步印证了竹子研究范围的局限性。因此，B段是正确答案。
6

6 examples of the uses to which bamboo is put
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：Modern processing techniques mean it can be used in a variety of ways, for example as flooring and laminates. Traditionally it is used in construction, but one of the fastest growing bamboo products is paper – 25 per cent of paper produced in India is made from bamboo fibre.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是“竹子的用途的例子”，需要找到文章中列举竹子具体用途的部分。

定位关键信息：通过题目中的关键词“examples of the uses”（用途的例子）定位，在D段中找到明确列举竹子用途的句子。

解题思路：D段明确列举了竹子的多种用途，包括现代处理技术下的地板和层压板、传统的建筑用途以及纸张制造（印度25%的纸张由竹纤维制成）。这些具体用途的例子与题目要求完全匹配，因此D段是正确答案。
7

7 an explanation of how bamboo may contribute to the survival of range of plants
✅ 正确答案：C

原文答案句：‘Bamboo’s pattern of mass flowering and mass death leaves behind large areas of dry biomass that attract wildfire,’ says Kapos, ‘When these bum, they create patches of open ground far bigger than would be left by a fallen tree. Patchiness helps to preserve diversity because certain plant species do better during the early stages of regeneration when there are gaps in the canopy.’

对应段落：C段

审题：题目问的是哪一段落解释了竹子如何有助于多种植物的生存。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"contribute to the survival of range of plants"，可以在C段中找到相关的句子，其中提到了竹子在森林结构和动态中的作用，以及它如何通过创造开放空间来促进植物多样性。

解题思路：原文中C段的句子明确指出了竹子通过其开花和死亡的周期性模式，会留下大片干燥的生物燃料，这些区域容易引发野火，会形成比倒树留下的空地大得多的空地。这种斑块状分布有助于保护生物多样性，因为某些植物在再生初期的空隙中生长得更好。这与题目中关于竹子如何有助于多种植物生存的信息完全吻合，因此C段是正确答案。

Questions 8-11


Look at the following statements (Questions 8-11) and the list of people below. Match each statement with the correct person, A-D. Write the correct letter, A-D, in boxes 8-11 on your answer sheet. 


NB you may use any letter more than once.


List of people 

A Ian Redmond 

B Valerie Kapos 

C Chris Stapleton 

D Ray Townsend 

 


Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
8

8 Some people do not regard bamboo as an endangered plant species.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：In a lot of places, the people who live with bamboo don’t perceive it as being under threat in any way, says Kapos, ‘In fact, a lot of bamboo species are very invasive if they’ve been introduced.’  

对应段落：E段  

审题：题目问“有些人不认为竹子是一种濒危植物物种”，需要匹配对应的人。  

定位关键信息：题目中的关键词“do not regard bamboo as an endangered plant species”可以定位到G段中“don’t perceive it as being under threat in any way”，与题目意思一致。  

解题思路：E段中直接引用了Valerie Kapos的话，提到“生活在竹子附近的人们并不认为竹子有任何威胁”，并补充说明“很多竹子物种如果被引入，会非常具有侵略性”，这与题目“有些人不认为竹子是濒危植物”吻合，因此答案为B（Valerie Kapos）。
9

9 A scarcity of bamboo places certain wildlife under threat.
✅ 正确答案：A

原文答案句：For the 650 or so that remain in the wild, it’s a vital food source. Without it, says Ian Redmond, chairman of the Ape Alliance, their chances of survival would be reduced significantly.

对应段落：A段

审题：题目问的是“竹子的稀缺使某些野生动物受到威胁”。

定位关键信息：利用题目中的关键词“scarcity of bamboo”和“certain wildlife under threat”定位到B段，找到Ian Redmond说的话，其中明确提到竹子是野生动物的重要食物来源，没有竹子，它们的生存机会将大大减少。

解题思路：原文中Ian Redmond的话明确指出竹子对于野生动物的生存至关重要，没有竹子会导致它们的生存机会减少，这与题目描述的“竹子的稀缺使某些野生动物受到威胁”完全一致。因此，答案为A。
10

10 Research methods investigating endangered plants have yet to be fully developed.
✅ 正确答案：B

原文答案句：‘People have only started looking at this during the past 10-15 years, and only now are they understanding how to go about it systematically,’ says Dr Valerie Kapos, one of the report’s authors. 

对应段落：B段  

审题：题目要求将“研究濒危植物的方法尚未完全成熟”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"Research methods"和"endangered plants"，可以在B段中找到相关句子。  

解题思路：段落B中，Valerie Kapos在谈论评估植物保护状况的科学时指出：“人们只是在过去10到15年间才开始关注这个问题，并且直到现在才开始理解如何系统地着手进行。” 这句话明确表示，针对植物（包括竹子）的保护研究方法本身仍处于早期发展和完善阶段，尚未成熟。因此，提出此观点的人是Valerie Kapos，对应选项B。
11

11 The greatest danger to bamboo is a disturbance of the places it grows in.
✅ 正确答案：D

原文答案句：‘There are two separate issues here’, says Ray Townsend, arboretum manager at the Royal Botanic Gardens, ‘Some plants are threatened because they can’t survive in the habitat – they aren’t strong enough or there aren’t enough of them, perhaps. But bamboo can take care of itself – it’s strong enough to survive if left alone. What is under threat is its habitat. When forest goes, it’s converted into something else; then there isn’t anywhere for forest plants such as bamboo to grow.’

对应段落：D段

审题：题目要求将“竹子面临的最大危险是其生长环境受到干扰”这一陈述与列表中对应的人物匹配。

定位关键信息：使用 "greatest danger", "disturbance of the places it grows in" 定位到文中明确指出竹子主要威胁是其栖息地丧失的句子。

解题思路：原文中Ray Townsend明确指出竹子本身很强壮，能够自保，但真正受到威胁的是它的栖息地，森林被破坏后竹子就没有生长的地方了。这与题目描述“竹子生长的地方受到破坏是最大的危险”完全一致，因此这句话是D说的。

Questions 12-13


Answer the questions below. Choose NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS from the passage for each answer. Write your answers in boxes 12 and 13 on your answer sheet.

 


12

12 What ecological problem do the roots of bamboo help to control?

正确答案： 12 soil erosion##Soil Erosion##SOIL EROSION
Question 12

原文答案句：Its rhizome systems, which lie in the top layers of the soil, are crucial in preventing soil erosion.  

对应段落：C段  

审题：题目问竹子的根部有助于控制哪种生态问题。  

定位关键信息：根据题干中的"ecological problem"和"roots of bamboo"定位到C段，该段提到竹子的根系系统（rhizome systems）在土壤表层，并起到防止某种生态问题的作用。  

解题思路：原文明确指出竹子的根系系统对于"preventing soil erosion"（防止土壤侵蚀）至关重要，因此答案为"soil erosion"。题干中的"ecological problem"对应原文的"soil erosion"，而"roots of bamboo"对应"rhizome systems"。


13

13 Which bamboo product is undergoing market expansion?

正确答案： 13 paper##Paper##PAPER
Question 13

原文答案句：Traditionally it is used in construction, but one of the fastest growing bamboo products is paper – 25 per cent of paper produced in India is made from bamboo fibre.

对应段落：D段

审题：题目问的是哪款竹制品正在进行市场拓展。

定位关键信息：根据题目中的关键词"market expansion"（市场扩张）定位到D段，该段提到"one of the fastest growing bamboo products is paper"（增长最快的竹产品之一是纸张），其中的"fastest growing"与"market expansion"对应。

解题思路：原文明确指出纸张是增长最快的竹产品之一，并且提到印度有25%的纸张是用竹纤维制造的，这表明纸张市场正在扩张。因此，答案为"paper"。
